general.allowedOriginsHint: One domain per line. Supports wildcard (e.g., *.example.com matches all subdomains) and exact domain (e.g., www.example.com). The site's own domain is always allowed; leave empty to only allow the site domain. Requests without Origin/Referer headers (e.g., server-side SDKs) are always permitted.

goals.title: Goals
goals.description: Set up conversion goals to track user actions like signups, purchases, or page visits.
goals.createFirst: Create your first goal to start tracking conversions.
goals.createDescription: Add a new conversion goal to track user actions.
goals.dialogDescription: Add a new conversion goal to track user actions.
goals.eventNameHint: The name of the custom event to track. Supports * wildcard (e.g., signup* matches signup_form, signup_button).
goals.pagePathHint: The page path to track. Supports * (match any characters) and ? (match single character). e.g., /soft/*.html matches /soft/1.html, /soft/abc.html.
goals.customPropsHint: Optional key-value pairs sent alongside the conversion event. Used to filter goal conversions in funnel analysis.
goals.propKeyHint: Select from existing keys or type a new one and press Enter.
goals.noAvailableProps: No custom property keys found. You can type a new key above.

stats.title: Imported Data Overview
stats.noData: No imported data found for the selected date range.

hero.title: ZenStats Documentation
hero.description: ZenStats is a self-hosted, privacy-first web analytics platform. Collect visitor data without third-party involvement, query it through a clean REST API, and stay fully in control of your infrastructure.

params.compare: Set to previous_period to include comparison values.
params.dateRange: Array of two dates [start, end] in YYYY-MM-DD format.
params.dimensionsV2: Array of dimension keys to group results by.
params.filtersV2: Array of filter tuples [operator, dimension, values].
params.offset: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

baseUrl.heading: Base URL
baseUrl.description: All API requests are made relative to your ZenStats instance URL.

responseFormat.heading: Response format
responseFormat.description: All endpoints return a consistent JSON envelope.

authentication.description: ZenStats supports two authentication methods. Choose based on your use case.

apiKey.heading: API Key
apiKey.description: Create an API key from your account settings. Pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
apiKey.callout: API keys grant read access to your analytics data. Store them securely and rotate them periodically from Settings → API Keys.

jwt.heading: JWT (dashboard sessions)
jwt.description: The web dashboard authenticates via short-lived JWT tokens issued at login. The token is refreshed automatically using a refresh token. This method is used internally by the dashboard and is not required for API integrations.

trackingScript.description: Add a single <script> tag to every page you want to track. No cookies are set by default.
trackingScript.note: Replace example.com with the domain you registered in ZenStats. The script is less than 2 KB gzipped.

customEvents.heading: Custom events
customEvents.description: Track user interactions beyond pageviews using zenstats.track().

engagement.heading: Engagement tracking
engagement.description: ZenStats automatically collects engagement time and scroll depth when the visitor leaves the page. These power the time_on_page and scroll_depth metrics without any additional configuration.

outbound.heading: Outbound link tracking
outbound.description: Add the data-zenstats-outbound attribute to any link to automatically track clicks as custom events named Outbound Link: Click.

aggregate.description: Returns aggregated metrics for a site over a given time period. Optionally compare against a previous period.

breakdown.description: Returns a ranked breakdown of a dimension. Use this to build top-pages, top-sources, and country tables.

timeseries.description: Returns time-series data points for the main graph. Granularity is automatically chosen based on the period (hourly for day, daily for longer ranges).

currentVisitors.description: Returns the count of visitors active on the site within the last 5 minutes. Useful for a live indicator.

v2Query.description: A more flexible query API that accepts a JSON body. Supports multi-dimensional grouping, ordering, and offset-based pagination.

metrics.description: Metrics can be combined freely in most API calls. Some metrics (time_on_page, scroll_depth) require engagement tracking to be active.

dimensions.description: Dimensions define what to group by in breakdown and v2 queries. Use the prefixed form (visit:source) in API calls.

filters.description: Filters narrow results to a subset of traffic. Pass them as a JSON array, URL-encoded in GET requests or as a JSON field in POST requests.

periods.description: Pass the period key as the period query parameter. For custom ranges, use the date_range field in the v2 API.

goals.description: Goals measure how many visitors complete a specific action — a pageview or a custom event. Configure them in Site Settings → Goals.
goals.callout: Goal conversions appear in the Stats dashboard under the Goals tab once configured.

create.heading: Creating a goal
create.description: In Site Settings → Goals, click Add Goal. Choose between a Pageview goal (triggered when a visitor views a URL matching a pattern) or an Event goal (triggered when your tracking script fires a named event).

types.heading: Goal types
types.pageviewDesc: Triggered when a visitor views a URL that matches a pattern. Supports wildcards, e.g. /blog/*.
types.eventDesc: Triggered when zenstats.track("EventName") is called. The event name must match exactly.

funnels.description: Funnels show step-by-step conversion through a sequence of pages or events. Each step must be a defined goal.
funnels.callout: Funnel data is computed in real time — there is no pre-aggregation. For very high-traffic sites, limit the date range to keep query times low.

create.heading: Creating a funnel
create.description: In Site Settings → Funnels, add a funnel and select at least two goals as steps. Steps are evaluated in order — a visitor must complete step N before being counted in step N+1.

segments.description: Segments are reusable filter sets. Save a combination of filters as a named segment and apply it across the dashboard without re-entering the same filters.

management.heading: Segment API
management.list: List all segments for the site.

sharedLinks.description: Shared links let you share a read-only view of your stats with people who do not have a ZenStats account. The link contains a unique slug; anyone with the URL can view the stats.
sharedLinks.callout: Shared link views pass the slug as a ?slug= query parameter to all API calls. No authentication token is required.

create.heading: Creating a shared link
create.description: In Site Settings → Shared Links, click Create Link, give it a name, and copy the generated URL.

health.description: Use these endpoints in your container orchestration health checks. /live returns 200 when the process is running; /ready returns 200 when all backing services (ClickHouse, PostgreSQL) are reachable.

deployment.description: ZenStats ships as a set of Docker images. The recommended way to run it is with the official docker-compose.yml from the zenstats-deploy repository.

quickStart.heading: Quick Start
quickStart.step2: Copy and edit the environment file

envVars.heading: Key Environment Variables
envVars.secretKey: JWT signing secret. Required in production. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32
envVars.domain: Public hostname of your ZenStats instance (e.g. stats.example.com). Used in the tracking script snippet.
envVars.dbPassword: PostgreSQL password. Change from the default before deploying.
envVars.maxmindKey: MaxMind GeoLite2 license key for GeoIP resolution. Leave empty to use the Loyalsoldier fallback database.
envVars.logLevel: Log verbosity. One of: debug, info, warn, error. Default: info.

services.heading: Services
services.frontend: Caddy-based reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API calls. Handles TLS automatically.
services.backend: Go API server. Handles event ingestion, authentication, and analytics queries.
services.postgres: PostgreSQL — stores users, sites, goals, funnels, and configuration.
services.clickhouse: ClickHouse — stores raw pageview and event data for analytics queries.

deployment.callout: Data is persisted in named Docker volumes (db-data, event-data, zenstats-data). Back these up regularly.

shields.description: Traffic shields let you filter out unwanted traffic before it reaches your analytics. Configure them in Site Settings → Shields.

ip.heading: IP Blacklist
ip.description: Block incoming events from specific IPv4 or IPv6 addresses. Useful for excluding your own office IP or known bots.

hostname.heading: Hostname Allowlist
hostname.description: Only accept events where the request hostname matches an allowed entry. Wildcards are supported at the start (e.g. *.example.com). Leave the list empty to allow all hostnames.

country.heading: Country Blacklist
country.description: Block all traffic from specific countries using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (e.g. CN, RU). Requires GeoIP to be configured.

referrer.heading: Spam Referrer Blacklist
referrer.description: Block events whose Referer header matches a known spam domain. Add domains one at a time.

shields.callout: Shield rules are evaluated on every inbound event before it is written to ClickHouse. Blocked events are dropped silently and do not appear in your analytics.

emailReports.description: ZenStats can send automated summary reports to site owners. Reports compare the current period against the previous one and include top pages, sources, and key metrics.

weekly.heading: Weekly Report
weekly.description: Sent every Monday at 8:00 AM (server timezone). Covers the previous full week.

monthly.heading: Monthly Report
monthly.description: Sent on the 1st of each month at 9:00 AM (server timezone). Covers the previous full month.

emailReports.callout: Email reports require SMTP to be configured in Admin → Settings. Toggle them on per site in Site Settings → Email Reports.

trafficAlerts.description: Traffic alerts notify you by email when your site traffic changes significantly compared to the same time last week.

settings.heading: Configuration
settings.threshold: Percentage change required to trigger an alert. For example, a threshold of 30 fires when traffic increases or decreases by more than 30%.
settings.interval: Comparison granularity: hourly (last hour vs. same hour last week) or daily (today vs. same day last week).
settings.recipients: Comma-separated list of additional email recipients. The site owner is always notified.

trafficAlerts.callout: Traffic alerts require SMTP to be configured in Admin → Settings. Enable them per site in Site Settings → Traffic Alerts.

metrics.bounceRate: Percentage of sessions with a single pageview.
metrics.timeOnPage: Average time spent on a page before navigating away.
metrics.scrollDepth: Average percentage of the page scrolled.
metrics.exitRate: Percentage of sessions that ended on this page.

filters.is: Exact match. Accepts multiple values (OR logic within one filter).

periods.custom: Custom date range. Use date_range in the v2 API.
